articles.title: Rochdale Youth Service Hosts Annual Youth Pride in the Park Event
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Youth Service's annual Youth Pride in the Park event attracted over 250 young attendees at Queen’s Park, Heywood, last week, featuring music, games, and activities.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Youth Service held its annual Youth Pride in the Park event at Queen’s Park, Heywood, last week. The event saw more than 250 young people in attendance, enjoying a range of activities.

Participants enjoyed music, games, and various other activities throughout the evening, which was marked by pleasant weather. The event provided a vibrant atmosphere for attendees to engage with one another.

The event aims to celebrate diversity and promote inclusivity among young people in the community. It serves as an important platform for fostering connections and support among attendees.

This gathering allowed young people to connect and engage in a supportive environment, reinforcing the values of acceptance and community spirit.

articles.title: Roadworks on Pilsworth Road in Heywood to Begin on 17 August 2026
articles.excerpt: Pilsworth Road in Heywood will undergo roadworks starting from 17 August 2026, as announced by Rochdale Borough Council.
articles.content_html:
Pilsworth Road in Heywood will undergo roadworks starting from 17 August 2026, according to Rochdale Borough Council.

The council has not provided specific details regarding the duration of the works or any expected disruptions to traffic.

Residents and motorists are advised to plan their journeys accordingly, as the roadworks may affect local traffic patterns.

Further updates will be issued by the council as the start date approaches.

articles.title: Rochdale's STARS Adult Care Service Rated Good by Inspectors
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Borough Council's STARS adult care service has received a 'good' rating from the Care Quality Commission following its latest inspection, recognising its effective support for vulnerable residents.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Borough Council's STARS adult care service has been rated 'good' by the Care Quality Commission (CQC) following its latest inspection. The service supports over 1,200 vulnerable residents each year after they leave hospital, helping them maintain independence at home.

The CQC assessment found the STARS service to be well-led, delivering effective, caring, and responsive support for people living in Heywood, Middleton, the Pennines, and Rochdale. Inspectors praised staff for treating residents with kindness, empathy, and compassion, while assisting them in living healthier lives and maintaining their independence, choice, and control.

The report highlighted the service's commitment to placing people at the centre of decisions regarding their care, effective partnership working, and strong communication with families and external services. The STARS service has been operational since 2008 and has also contributed to reducing health inequalities and freeing up valuable hospital beds.

Councillor Shah Wazir, the council's cabinet member for adult care, expressed pride in the service, stating that the CQC report is a testament to the high-quality care provided by the STARS team. He acknowledged the importance of delivering services that help people remain independent in their preferred environments. The council's wider adult care service also received a good rating from the CQC, further underscoring the quality of care in the borough.

articles.title: Twelve Green Spaces in Heywood Celebrate Green Flag Award Success
articles.excerpt: Twelve green spaces across Heywood, Rochdale, Middleton and the Pennines have received the Green Flag Award for another year, recognising the efforts of local teams and volunteers.
articles.content_html:
Twelve green spaces across Heywood, Rochdale, Middleton and the Pennines are celebrating another year of success in the Green Flag Award scheme. This accolade highlights the quality and management of these green spaces, which are vital to the local community.

The Green Flag Award is a national standard for parks and green spaces, recognising well-managed sites that meet specific criteria. The award is a testament to the hard work of local teams and volunteers who dedicate their time to maintaining these areas throughout the year.

The success of these green spaces contributes positively to the local environment and community well-being, providing residents with accessible areas for recreation and relaxation.

This achievement reflects the ongoing commitment to enhancing the quality of life in the region, and it is hoped that the recognition will encourage further community involvement in maintaining and improving local green spaces.

articles.title: Heywood Town Environmental Update Announced
articles.excerpt: Heywood Town Council has unveiled a series of environmental initiatives designed to enhance local green spaces and promote sustainability within the community. The plans include the planting of additional trees, the installation of new recycling bins, and the organisation of community clean-up events.
articles.content_html:
Heywood Town Council has unveiled a series of environmental initiatives designed to enhance local green spaces and promote sustainability within the community.

The plans include the planting of additional trees, the installation of new recycling bins, and the organisation of community clean-up events.

Council members emphasised the importance of community involvement in these initiatives, encouraging residents to participate in upcoming events to help improve their local environment.

Further details about the initiatives and how residents can get involved will be shared on the council's official website and social media channels.

articles.title: Traffic Disruption on Heywood Lane Following Roadworks
articles.excerpt: Roadworks on Heywood Lane in Wilkesley, Whitchurch, are causing traffic delays. Motorists are advised to seek alternative routes during this time.
articles.content_html:
Roadworks on Heywood Lane in Wilkesley, Whitchurch, are currently causing traffic delays. The works began on 10 July 2026 and are expected to last for several days.

Motorists are advised to seek alternative routes to avoid congestion in the area. Local authorities have indicated that the road will remain partially closed during the works.

The roadworks are part of ongoing maintenance efforts aimed at improving road safety and infrastructure in the region. Drivers should remain cautious and adhere to any signage in place.
